NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an open-source AI assistant designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to autonomously create and refine skills from experience while maintaining user models and session history. The system supports 14 messaging platforms and six execution backends, including Docker and SSH, allowing it to run on diverse infrastructure from local machines to serverless cloud environments. Users can integrate over 200 models through various providers and automate workflows using a built-in natural language cron scheduler.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement through a closed learning loop.
Supports 14 platforms including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp.
Six execution backends including Docker, SSH, and serverless Modal persistence.

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: This repository provides the implementation and examples for enabling function calling and JSON mode in Hermes 2 Pro models. It utilizes a structured recursive loop to generate tool calls, execute them via Python scripts, and feed the results back into the model for final responses. Users can define custom tools using Pydantic models or modify existing financial data functions powered by the yfinance library. The system is designed for OpenAI-compatible workflows using the ChatML prompt format to ensure reliable multi-turn interactions.
highlights: Implements recursive function calling and structured JSON mode inference
Supports custom tool definition via Pydantic and OpenAI-style schemas
Uses ChatML format for steerable multi-turn agentic dialogues

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning with large language models. It operates by hosting environments as services that interact with a trajectory API to collect, distribute, and evaluate model data for training loops. The framework supports diverse interactive settings including tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal tasks. It has been used to train specialized models like DeepHermes for complex multi-turn interactions and financial prediction.
highlights: Microservice architecture for asynchronous RL environment management
Supports tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal training environments
Standardized trajectory API for data collection and batch processing

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. The system utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, prompts, and tool descriptions. It validates candidate improvements through a rigorous pipeline of synthetic or session-based evaluations, constraint gates, and mandatory test suites. The final output is a refined version of the agent's components delivered via pull requests for human review.
highlights: Optimizes skills and prompts using reflective evolutionary search via API calls.
Analyzes execution traces to understand and fix specific failure points.
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates.

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that integrates Hermes Agent into the Paperclip company management system as an automated employee. It functions by wrapping the Hermes CLI, parsing raw terminal output into structured transcript objects that Paperclip renders as interactive tool cards and markdown. This integration allows Hermes to respond to task assignments and issue comments while utilizing its full suite of 30+ native tools and persistent memory. The adapter also manages session state migration, cost tracking, and automatic skill synchronization between the two platforms.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agent to Paperclip as a managed virtual employee
Parses raw CLI output into structured tool cards and markdown
Supports 8 inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

outsourc-e/hermes-workspace.summary: Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to manage and orchestrate AI agents through a unified graphical interface. It functions as a Progressive Web App that connects to OpenAI-compatible backends or the Hermes gateway to provide real-time SSE streaming of agent outputs and tool calls. The platform integrates a terminal, file browser, and an inspector for searching and editing agent memory and skills. It supports local model providers like Ollama and LM Studio, offering enhanced session persistence and job tracking when routed through a dedicated gateway.
highlights: Unified interface for chat, terminal, memory browser, and skills management
Real-time SSE streaming with visual rendering for agent tool calls
Supports local backends including Ollama, Atomic Chat, and vLLM

fathah/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native GUI companion designed to simplify the installation, configuration, and management of the Hermes Agent AI assistant. It replaces manual CLI management by providing a guided setup process and a centralized interface for interacting with the agent. Users can manage multiple LLM providers, configure messaging gateways, and control complex toolsets through a visual workspace. The application also includes features for session history, memory management, and scheduled task automation.
highlights: Guided installation and dependency resolution for Hermes Agent
Support for diverse LLM providers and local endpoints
Integrated messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram

sanchomuzax/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a dedicated process monitoring and configuration dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files to provide real-time updates without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can track session histories, monitor platform health, manage scheduled cron jobs, and inspect skill source code through a responsive, themeable interface. The system includes built-in cost tracking and full-text search for conversation logs to simplify agent oversight.
highlights: Real-time dashboard for session activity, gateway status, and cost tracking
Full-text search and inspection of conversation histories via SQLite
Read-only configuration viewing and management of scheduled cron jobs

Euraika-Labs/pan-ui.summary: Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed specifically for the Hermes Agent runtime. It provides a centralized interface to manage agent interactions through a chat-first environment that includes session management, tool timelines, and approval workflows. The platform integrates directly with the Hermes API and filesystem to handle skill discovery via skills.sh, MCP server configuration, and profile-scoped memory management. Users can deploy the interface as a background daemon or system service to maintain a persistent management layer for their AI agents.
highlights: Unified dashboard for chat, memory management, and agent profile isolation
Integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers
Automated setup wizard with support for daemon and systemd execution

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.summary: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is a library of 754 structured security skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific workflows of a senior analyst. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard, providing YAML-frontmatter for discovery and structured Markdown for step-by-step execution across 26 security domains. Each skill is uniquely mapped to five industry frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. This allows agents to perform complex tasks like threat hunting, cloud forensics, and malware analysis with built-in compliance and technical context.
highlights: 754 structured skills across 26 specialized security domains
Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks
Compatible with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and agentskills.io platforms

conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing.summary: Avoid-ai-writing is a portable skill for AI agents designed to audit and rewrite text to eliminate detectable machine-generated patterns. It utilizes a structured two-pass process and a 109-entry word replacement table to replace common "AI-isms" with plainer, more human-like language. The tool offers both a rewrite mode for active editing and a detection-only mode for identifying issues without altering the source. It is compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw via the agentskills.io standard.
highlights: Identifies 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells
Uses a three-tier word replacement table for precise editing
Performs a second-pass audit to catch surviving linguistic patterns

wondelai/skills.summary: Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.
highlights: Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills.summary: pydantic-ai-skills is a framework that implements the Agent Skills open specification to provide modular, specialized capabilities for Pydantic AI agents. It uses a tool-calling approach to enable progressive disclosure, allowing agents to discover and load detailed instructions or scripts only when relevant to a task. The package supports skills defined as filesystem directories or created programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses. This architecture reduces token consumption by preventing the injection of unnecessary context into the initial system prompt.
highlights: Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

Agents365-ai/drawio-skill.summary: This project is a diagramming skill that enables AI agents to generate professional draw.io XML files and image exports from natural language descriptions. It utilizes the native draw.io desktop CLI to produce grid-aligned diagrams with specialized presets for architectures, UML, and machine learning models. The tool features an iterative self-check loop that automatically identifies and fixes layout issues while supporting animated connectors for data-flow visualization. It integrates with major agent platforms including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw.
highlights: Generates editable .drawio files and exports to PNG, SVG, or PDF
Includes 6 diagram presets including ML models with tensor shape annotations
Automated self-check loop identifies and fixes six common layout issues

smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills.summary: Chainlink Agent Skills provides a standardized set of capabilities for AI agents to interact with decentralized oracle networks and smart contracts. The project implements the Agent Skills specification, using YAML frontmatter and markdown instructions to define specific workflows. It currently offers specialized skills for Chainlink Runtime Environment (CRE) operations and Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) tasks like token transfers and local testing. These skills can be integrated into agent environments using the Vercel skills installer and invoked via explicit chat commands.
highlights: Standardized skills for Chainlink Runtime Environment and CCIP workflows
Follows the agentskills.io specification using YAML and markdown
Supports CCIP contract discovery, monitoring, and cross-chain sends

tlehman/litprog-skill.summary: The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.
highlights: Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

esaradev/icarus-plugin.summary: Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.
highlights: Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

chigwell/skilldock.io.summary: SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to search, install, and version-control modular agent capabilities. The platform supports a dual-rail commerce system using TON and Stripe for buying and selling skills, alongside recursive dependency resolution for local installations. Developers can publish private or public skills by uploading zipped packages containing metadata and versioned descriptions.
highlights: OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill discovery and management
Supports recursive dependency resolution and local skill installation
Integrated commerce features for skill monetization via TON and Stripe

black-forest-labs/skills.summary: This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.
highlights: Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory.summary: Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.
highlights: Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

ReinaMacCredy/maestro.summary: Maestro is a local-first conductor designed to coordinate multi-agent software engineering workflows through a shared on-disk state model. It replaces ephemeral chat history with a disciplined structure in a local .maestro directory, allowing separate agent sessions to collaborate without requiring a server or database. The system uses a plan-approve-execute lifecycle where a human operator manages missions, milestones, and features across multiple terminals. By utilizing UKI v5.4 handoffs and structured memory, it enables agents to pass compact context and reusable learnings between sessions.
highlights: Maintains shared state on disk via a local .maestro directory
Facilitates agent-to-agent context transfers using structured UKI v5.4 handoffs
Provides a read-only TUI and JSON snapshots for mission tracking

tiann/execplan-skill.summary: ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.
highlights: Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill.summary: Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client and daemon architecture designed to minimize token consumption in agentic workflows. It implements a three-layer progressive disclosure pattern that loads server metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required by the AI. This approach reduces context window usage by up to 86% compared to loading entire toolsets upfront. The project includes a CLI for managing persistent MCP connections and supports hot-reloading configurations without restarting the background daemon.
highlights: Three-layer lazy loading reduces token usage for tool definitions
Socket-based daemon maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers
CLI commands for metadata retrieval, tool listing, and schema inspection

armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.summary: Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discussions into verified instruction files for AI agents to prevent API hallucinations. It uses AST extraction to map function signatures and types directly to specific source files and line numbers, ensuring agents follow real implementation details. The tool generates version-pinned skills compliant with the agentskills.io specification, supporting workflows for libraries, SaaS APIs, and complex dependency stacks. Every skill includes a provenance map for auditability and a compact context snippet for efficient agent memory management.
highlights: Extracts verified function signatures and types via AST analysis
Links every AI instruction to specific source file line numbers
Generates agentskills.io-compliant markdown and JSON instruction sets

amanning3390/hermeshub.summary: HermesHub is a curated registry and marketplace for AI agent skills built on the agentskills.io open standard. It provides a centralized platform for browsing, searching, and installing verified capabilities for Hermes Agent via a command-line interface. The system utilizes an automated security scanner with over 65 rules to detect threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration before skills are listed. It also features a creator marketplace supporting crypto-based micropayments and an agent-to-agent feedback protocol for building trust scores.
highlights: Automated security scanning for prompt injection and malicious payloads
One-click installation via Hermes Agent command-line interface
Creator marketplace with x402 and Micropayment Protocol support

PederHP/skillsdotnet.summary: SkillsDotNet is a C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification that enables the distribution and discovery of agent skills via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It utilizes a "context-as-progressive-disclosure" approach, where agents initially load lightweight frontmatter for discovery and fetch full instructions on demand. The library integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI to provide automated tool calling for loading skills and supports vendor-specific skill directories from platforms like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot. It also includes a dependency management system that can trigger on-demand connections to required MCP servers.
highlights: Implements agentskills.io specification for C# and .NET environments
Provides MCP resource templates for on-demand skill and file loading
Includes vendor shortcuts for Claude, Cursor, and Copilot skill directories

vectorize-io/hindsight.summary: Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to help AI agents learn and evolve over time rather than just recalling conversation history. It utilizes biomimetic data structures to organize information into world facts, mental models, and episodic memories, moving beyond standard vector search or knowledge graphs. The system provides a retain-recall-reflect workflow that allows agents to store new information, search existing memories, and generate disposition-aware responses. It integrates via an LLM wrapper or SDK and supports major providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
highlights: Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term learning
Uses biomimetic data structures to organize facts and mental models
Offers a two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management

greyhaven-ai/autocontext.summary: autocontext is a recursive self-improvement harness designed to prevent LLM agents from starting every task with a cold context. It uses a structured multi-agent loop—comprising competitors, analysts, and coaches—to evaluate agent runs, extract validated lessons, and persist them as reusable playbooks. The system supports diverse execution surfaces including verifier-driven missions, evidence-based investigations, and parameter-sweep simulations. Successful behaviors can eventually be distilled from frontier models into smaller, local runtimes using MLX.
highlights: Multi-agent loop extracts and persists validated knowledge across repeated runs
Supports eleven scenario families including negotiation, workflows, and tool fragility
Distills stable agent behaviors into local models via MLX integration

elkimek/honcho-self-hosted.summary: This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.
highlights: Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

yoloshii/ClawMem.summary: ClawMem is a local-first memory and context layer designed to provide persistent knowledge for AI agents across different sessions and platforms. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval architecture that combines vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant documents, decisions, and causal relationships. The system automatically captures insights from conversation transcripts using local models and maintains a shared SQLite vault accessible by Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents. By operating entirely on-device without cloud dependencies, it ensures data privacy while enabling cross-runtime memory continuity.
highlights: Hybrid retrieval combining vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal
Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
Cross-platform memory sharing between Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes

plur-ai/plur.summary: PLUR is a local-first persistent memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and utilizes a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25 search and BGE embeddings without requiring external API calls. The system uses an activation model where memories strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to ensure context remains relevant. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and injection of learned information.
highlights: Stores persistent memory locally as open-format YAML engrams
Hybrid retrieval using BM25 and embeddings with zero API costs
Automatic memory decay and strengthening based on usage frequency

amanning3390/flowstate-qmd.summary: FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents from a SQLite-based vector store before an agent initiates a reactive search loop. The system runs locally using GGUF models and provides an MCP-native interface for integration with tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes. This approach allows agents to access design decisions, changelogs, and migration notes without waiting for manual tool calls during a session.
highlights: Anticipatory cache prefetches context into a local intuition file
Local-first architecture using SQLite-vec and node-llama-cpp for privacy
MCP server integration for seamless use with coding agent workflows

robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus.summary: Web-search-plus is a multi-provider search plugin for Hermes Agent that consolidates various search engines into a single tool. It uses an intelligent auto-routing engine to select the most appropriate provider, such as Serper for news or Exa for semantic research, based on query intent and linguistic signals. The plugin features adaptive fallback mechanisms and local caching to ensure reliability and performance even when specific APIs are on cooldown. Users can also manually filter results by time range and domain or utilize specialized deep-research modes for complex document analysis.
highlights: Supports seven providers including Serper, Tavily, Exa, and Perplexity
Intelligent auto-routing selects providers based on query intent
Includes local caching and automatic provider fallback mechanisms

42-evey/hermes-plugins.summary: Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent. The suite implements a modular architecture featuring multi-model debate systems, smart model routing with fallback chains, and structured JSON telemetry for self-inspection. These tools enable agents to manage long-term goals, consolidate memories into vector storage, and maintain bidirectional communication with external environments like Claude Code and Telegram.
highlights: Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus decision-making tools.
Budget enforcement and cost tracking via Langfuse integration.
Adaptive memory management with nightly fact consolidation and importance scoring.

FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin.summary: The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.
highlights: Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin.summary: The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.
highlights: Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

nativ3ai/hermes-payguard.summary: Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.
highlights: Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare.summary: This plugin integrates Cloudflare Browser Rendering into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable advanced web interaction and data extraction. It leverages Cloudflare's headless browser API to handle JavaScript-heavy websites that traditional scrapers often fail to process. The toolset includes capabilities for automated crawling, CSS-based scraping, and AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3. Users can also generate full-page screenshots, PDFs, and clean Markdown versions of web content.
highlights: Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
Captures full-page screenshots and converts web pages to PDF or Markdown

builderz-labs/mission-control.summary: Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to manage and coordinate fleets of AI agents. It provides a centralized interface for dispatching tasks, tracking costs, and monitoring multi-agent workflows using a local-first SQLite architecture. The platform includes built-in quality gates for human-in-the-loop review and supports recurring task scheduling via natural language. It integrates with various agent frameworks such as CrewAI, LangGraph, and Claude Code to provide unified observability without external database dependencies.
highlights: Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
Real-time task monitoring via WebSocket and SSE push updates
Built-in quality gates and Aegis review system for task sign-off

swarmclawai/swarmclaw.summary: SwarmClaw is a self-hosted runtime designed for orchestrating and managing autonomous AI agent swarms. It operates as a multi-provider system that supports MCP-native tools, delegation, and scheduled tasks across various LLM backends and CLI-based agents. The platform enables agents to learn new skills from reviewed conversations and provides an organizational chart interface to visualize team activity. It integrates with over 20 providers, including OpenClaw, Hermes, and local models via Ollama.
highlights: Self-hosted runtime for autonomous agent orchestration and delegation
Supports 23 providers including Hermes, OpenClaw, and local Ollama
Features conversation-to-skill learning and persistent agent memory

howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness.summary: Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system automates benchmark orchestration, archive analysis, and frontier tracking to identify high-performing harness candidates. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, providing structured mutation and search capabilities to refine agentic workflows.
highlights: Optimizes harness code through an automated outer-loop search process
Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluation via Hermes Agent
Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

runtimenoteslabs/gladiator.summary: Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.
highlights: Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent.summary: Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI that automates complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to coordinate 17 specialized agents across research, planning, implementation, quality, documentation, and infrastructure categories. The system operates through strict pipeline architectures that enforce mandatory quality gates, security audits, and user checkpoints. It integrates with various LLM models and MCP servers to handle tasks ranging from feature development to performance optimization.
highlights: Coordinates 17 specialized agents through a master orchestrator pipeline
Enforces mandatory quality gates with reviewer and tester agent loops
Supports specialized workflows for security, refactoring, and infrastructure tasks

supermodeltools/bigiron.summary: Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.
highlights: Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill.summary: The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.
highlights: Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

numtide/llm-agents.nix.summary: llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI coding agents and development tools. It provides a unified repository of Nix expressions that automate the installation of various terminal-based agents, ranging from local open-source tools to proprietary binaries. The project is updated daily to ensure users have access to the latest versions of tools like Goose, Claude Code, and various agent orchestrators. This infrastructure allows developers to run diverse AI agents instantly via Nix flakes without manual environment configuration.
highlights: Provides Nix packages for numerous terminal-based AI coding agents
Automates daily updates for all included agentic development tools
Supports both open-source and proprietary binary-based AI tools

TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within a hardware-enforced sandbox using NVIDIA OpenShell. It secures agent operations by intercepting system calls at the kernel level to block unauthorized network egress, filesystem writes, and dangerous syscalls. The system features three security policy presets—strict, gateway, and permissive—allowing users to toggle access to external tools like Telegram, Discord, and web search without restarting the environment. It supports over 40 built-in tools, persistent memory, and a library of pre-built skills for automated research, code review, and system monitoring.
highlights: Hardware-level enforcement of network, filesystem, and process boundaries
Three security policy presets to control agent capabilities and access
Integrates with Telegram, Discord, and Slack via secure gateways

0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent.summary: nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.
highlights: Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows desktop implementation of the Hermes Agent designed for non-technical users to run AI agents without installation or admin rights. It utilizes a Tkinter GUI and an embedded Python environment to bridge local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The system features a workflow engine for automating multi-step pipelines and a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime. It also integrates portable servers for text-to-speech, music generation, and ComfyUI image generation.
highlights: 100 tools including local model management and GPU monitoring
No-install deployment requiring no admin rights or Docker
Integrated workflow engine for scheduled and conditional automation

xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker.summary: This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.
highlights: Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server.summary: This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.
highlights: Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes.summary: This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.
highlights: Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

raulvidis/hermes-android.summary: Hermes-android is a remote automation bridge that enables Hermes agents to control Android devices through a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset. The system establishes an outbound WebSocket connection from the phone to a server, bypassing the need for port forwarding or USB cables via a 6-character pairing code. It provides 36 specialized tools for UI interaction, including screen reading, gesture execution, and system-level actions like making calls or sending SMS. This integration allows agents to perform autonomous mobile tasks such as checking notifications, managing contacts, and navigating third-party applications.
highlights: Executes 36 tools for UI navigation, media control, and system actions
Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and coordinate-based gesture automation
Connects via WebSocket relay to work behind NAT and firewalls

teknium1/hermes-miniverse.summary: Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council.summary: Hermes-council is an MCP server designed to provide adversarial, multi-perspective decision-making for the hermes-agent ecosystem. It functions by orchestrating five distinct personas—Advocate, Skeptic, Oracle, Contrarian, and Arbiter—to debate complex questions through structured deliberation. The system produces calibrated verdicts with confidence scores and evidence links, and it can be used to generate DPO preference pairs for Reinforcement Learning training.
highlights: Five distinct intellectual personas for adversarial deliberation
Generates DPO preference pairs for RL training
Supports custom persona configuration via YAML

marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors.summary: Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.
highlights: Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle.summary: Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.
highlights: Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

junhoyeo/tokscale.summary: Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It operates by scanning local databases and session files from supported clients to aggregate usage data into a native Rust TUI or a web-based dashboard. The tool provides real-time pricing calculations using LiteLLM data, accounting for tiered pricing and cache discounts. It supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot CLI.
highlights: Tracks token usage across 20+ AI coding agents and CLIs
Provides real-time cost estimation using LiteLLM pricing data
Features a native Rust TUI and 3D contribution visualization

joeynyc/hermes-skins.summary: Hermes Skins is a collection of community-driven visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal interface's appearance. The project utilizes YAML configuration files to modify visual elements such as ASCII art banners, color schemes, spinner animations, and branding text without altering the agent's underlying logic. Users can apply themes like Skynet, Vault-Tec, or Sakura by placing files in a local directory and updating their configuration. This system supports partial overrides, meaning custom skins inherit missing values from a default template for easier creation.
highlights: Customizes CLI visuals including banners, spinners, and response-box labels
Uses YAML files for simple theme definition and inheritance
Includes diverse themes like Pirate, Vault-Tec, and Cyberdyne Systems

AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC.summary: hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.
highlights: Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser.summary: Vessel is an open-source Chromium-based browser designed for persistent web agents that require durable state and human supervision. It operates as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) host, allowing external agent harnesses like Hermes to control the browser while maintaining a visible UI for human auditing and intervention. The platform features named session persistence, structured page visibility context, and built-in tools for handling popups and consent walls. It bridges the gap between headless automation and manual browsing by providing a dedicated runtime for long-running autonomous workflows.
highlights: MCP-native control for external agent harnesses and Hermes Agent
Durable session persistence for cookies, bookmarks, and tab layouts
Human-in-the-loop supervisory interface with live agent transcripts and approvals

Abruptive/Ankh.md.summary: Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.
highlights: Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

roli-lpci/lintlang.summary: lintlang is a static linter designed to identify language-based bugs in AI agent configurations, tool descriptions, and system prompts. It uses seven structural detectors (H1-H7) to find issues like tool ambiguity and missing termination conditions without requiring LLM calls. The tool integrates into CI pipelines to gate deployments based on finding severity. It supports YAML, JSON, and plain text formats for a wide range of agent frameworks.
highlights: Static analysis with zero LLM calls, cost, or latency
Seven structural detectors for common agent failure modes
CI integration with verdict-based gating (PASS, REVIEW, FAIL)

0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes.summary: openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.
highlights: Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

42-evey/evey-setup.summary: Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.
highlights: Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

ucsandman/DashClaw.summary: DashClaw is a decision infrastructure layer designed to prevent AI agents from making costly or unauthorized mistakes by intercepting actions before execution. It functions as a gateway between agents and external systems, evaluating actions against guard policies and risk assessments in real-time. The platform records verifiable evidence for every decision, providing a replayable audit trail and cost analytics through token usage tracking. It supports multiple integration methods including an MCP server, framework-native plugins, and SDKs for environments like LangChain, CrewAI, and Claude Code.
highlights: Evaluates guard policies and risk before agent actions execute.
Provides an MCP server for zero-code governance in Claude environments.
Records verifiable evidence and decision replays for every agent action.

Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander.summary: Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity from P0 to P3, and apply corrective actions across CPU, memory, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.
highlights: Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions
Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically
Supports parallel subagent spawning for multi-layer service investigations

Christabel337/job-scout-agent.summary: Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.
highlights: Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

rodmarkun/anihermes.summary: AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.
highlights: Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-moni.summary: This project is a reference implementation for autonomous AI infrastructure monitoring using the Hermes Agent framework. It utilizes scheduled cron-based workflows to ingest research from arXiv and track trending repositories on GitHub to identify infrastructure risks and ecosystem shifts. The system automatically generates structured markdown reports, including cost projections, compute bottleneck alerts, and an ASCII monitoring dashboard. It serves as a template for developers building long-running, headless agentic systems for operational intelligence.
highlights: Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem tracking.
Cron-based workflows for cost modeling and infrastructure risk analysis.
Generates structured markdown reports and ASCII-style monitoring dashboards.

bigph00t/hermescraft.summary: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.
highlights: Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

hxsteric/mercury.summary: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.
highlights: Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

bryercowan/hermes-embodied.summary: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.
highlights: Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent.summary: Awesome Hermes Agent is a curated directory of resources and extensions for the Hermes Agent ecosystem developed by Nous Research. It categorizes community-contributed skills, tools, and integrations while providing maturity ratings to help users navigate the self-improving agent's capabilities. The repository serves as a central hub for discovering specialized plugins for goal management, autonomous novel writing, and production incident response. It also provides structured guidance for new users to transition from basic installation to advanced multi-agent orchestration.
highlights: Categorizes skills by production, beta, and experimental maturity levels
Indexes official Nous Research tools for self-evolution and novel writing
Lists community integrations for Telegram, Discord, and workspace GUIs

alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book.summary: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a comprehensive practical guide for the open-source AI Agent framework developed by Nous Research. It explains the framework's core architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loops, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides seventeen chapters of content ranging from initial installation to advanced multi-agent orchestration and development automation. It serves as a technical resource for developers transitioning from other tools like Claude Code or Cursor to the Hermes ecosystem.
highlights: Detailed breakdown of three-layer memory and self-improving learning loops
Step-by-step tutorials for installation and multi-platform customization
Real-world scenarios for knowledge assistants and development automation

OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide.summary: The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to move Hermes Agent deployments from basic installation to production-ready environments. It provides a 21-part instructional series alongside runnable artifacts, including a one-command VPS bootstrap script and pre-configured systemd units. The project includes 13 installable skills and 5 opinionated configuration templates tailored for specific needs like cost-optimization, security hardening, and Telegram bot integration. Users can implement advanced features such as MCP server orchestration, Langfuse observability, and remote sandboxing for coding agents.
highlights: One-command VPS bootstrap script for automated Debian and Ubuntu deployment
Thirteen ready-to-use skills and five specialized configuration templates
Reference architectures for homelabs, agencies, and mobile road warriors

metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu.summary: This project provides a comprehensive configuration guide and automated installation script for running Hermes Agent on Windows via WSL2 or natively on Linux and macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for local inference, utilizing Qwen3.5 GGUF models with hardware acceleration for CUDA and Metal. The setup includes essential components like the Camofox browser server for web automation and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. Users can deploy a fully local agentic workflow with OpenAI-compatible API support and Telegram bot integration.
highlights: Automated setup script for Hermes Agent, llama.cpp, and Camofox browser.
Supports GPU acceleration via CUDA for WSL2/Linux and Metal for macOS.
Includes detailed model selection matrix for Qwen3.5 GGUF quantizations.

mudrii/hermes-agent-docs.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform deployment across various messaging and IDE environments. It utilizes a closed learning loop that automatically creates and refines skills, manages long-term memory via SQLite, and performs full-text searches across past conversation sessions. The agent supports concurrent tool execution and integrates with dozens of model providers, including native support for Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and the Nous Portal. Its architecture allows for isolated subagents, scheduled cron jobs, and the export of training trajectories for model fine-tuning.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
Unified gateway for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and twelve other platforms
Extensible plugin architecture with lifecycle hooks and MCP server management

martymcenroe/HermesWiki.summary: HermesWiki is a centralized documentation repository designed to support users of the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It serves as a knowledge base that provides structured guidance on implementing and managing the agent's core functions. The project offers a collection of deployment patterns and practical recipes to streamline the setup process. By consolidating technical instructions, it helps developers navigate the specific configurations required for autonomous email workflows.
highlights: Official documentation hub for the Hermes AI email agent
Provides curated deployment patterns for various environments
Includes practical recipes for autonomous email task management

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a fork of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL trust boundaries to protect against indirect prompt injection. It works by separating trusted operator control from untrusted data sources like web content and tool outputs through a dedicated security envelope and capability gating. The system enforces a trusted operator plan derived from real user turns to authorize sensitive actions such as terminal execution and file mutation. Users can toggle between guarded, monitor, and legacy runtime modes to compare security enforcement against standard agent behavior.
highlights: Separates trusted operator control from untrusted tool and web data
Gates sensitive capabilities like terminal execution and file mutation
Implements provenance-aware wrapping for all untrusted model context

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.
highlights: Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions across coding, research, and system administration tasks to generate high-quality training data that reflects actual user interactions. The system automatically scores these trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery to create SFT-ready datasets or facilitate live reinforcement learning. By integrating with the Atropos training framework, it enables a closed loop where the agent's production performance informs the next model iteration.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from 30 diverse real-world task categories
Automated multi-dimensional reward scoring for task completion and efficiency
Direct integration with Atropos for live RL and GRPO training

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time system monitoring, multi-agent gateway management, and a browser-based terminal. Users can configure over 80 agent settings, track detailed token analytics across different models, and manage scheduled cron jobs for automated tasks. The platform also includes a revamped chat interface with collapsible tool call cards and a secure file explorer scoped to the agent's directory.
highlights: Multi-agent gateway for starting, stopping, and configuring individual profiles.
Detailed token analytics tracking costs and usage by model and platform.
Role-based access control with 28 permissions across 12 functional groups.

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a dedicated workspace for managing Hermes Agents over SSH. It connects directly to remote hosts to manage sessions, files, and cron jobs without requiring a browser wrapper, gateway API, or local data mirroring. The app features a multi-tabbed terminal, conflict-aware file editing, and profile-specific usage tracking. It is designed for users who need a high-performance, native interface while maintaining the remote host as the single source of truth.
highlights: Direct SSH connection with no local file mirroring or daemon required
Profile-aware workspace for managing sessions, skills, and cron jobs
Integrated multi-tabbed terminal with live theme and color controls

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a lightweight, dark-themed browser interface for the Hermes Agent, providing a graphical alternative to the command-line environment. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, it achieves 1:1 parity with the CLI without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing sessions, active chats, and workspace file browsing. It includes a dedicated control center for managing agent settings, model profiles, and persistent memory features.
highlights: Full parity with Hermes CLI features in a web interface
Three-panel layout for chat, sessions, and workspace file browsing
Lightweight architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript without bundlers

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is a persistent memory and context layer designed to transform AI agents into long-term knowledge repositories. It utilizes a local PGLite database and a library of 25 specialized skills to ingest, enrich, and cross-link data from meetings, emails, and social media. The system operates on a "brain-first" principle, requiring agents to query internal memory for context before executing external API calls or generating responses. This architecture enables autonomous background tasks like citation fixing, entity extraction, and automated daily briefings via MCP server integrations.
highlights: Provides 25 specialized skills for autonomous data ingestion and enrichment
Exposes 30+ MCP tools for integration with Claude and Cursor
Uses PGLite for zero-config local vector and relational storage

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface designed to provide a visual management layer for the self-hosted Hermes Agent. It operates as a single-file React application served via a lightweight Python proxy that handles real-time log streaming and API communication without external pip dependencies. The interface includes specialized panels for monitoring system stats, inspecting internal memory files, and browsing Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools. Users can also preview generated artifacts like HTML or SVG files in a sandboxed environment and manage their agent's skills and session history.
highlights: Single-file React architecture with no build steps or dependencies
Real-time log streaming and tool call visualization via SSE
Integrated file browser and memory inspector for agent configuration

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that enables Hermes Agent and OpenClaw to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It functions as a central iLink poller that creates two local proxy servers, allowing both gateways to receive messages without the 403 errors typically caused by token conflicts. Users can toggle between agents or receive responses from both simultaneously using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, and /both. The tool is a lightweight Python proxy that forwards raw protocol messages, ensuring native handling of media and voice transcriptions by each respective gateway.
highlights: Enables dual-agent operation on one WeChat iLink account
Routes messages via /hermes, /openclaw, or /both commands
Automated installation script patches existing gateway configurations

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) engine that persists every message in an immutable SQLite store. When context limits are reached, the system performs multi-level escalation from detailed summaries to deterministic truncation while maintaining retrieval paths. This allows the agent to use specialized tools like grep and expansion to drill back into original message content from compacted summaries.
highlights: Immutable SQLite storage ensures no conversation data is ever deleted
Hierarchical DAG structure enables multi-level summary escalation and retrieval
Built-in agent tools for searching, describing, and expanding compacted context
